data.body:
Goal:
Our lease forensics showed that Knots claim/poll prompts are telling agents to run `kno next ... --actor-kind agent --agent-name <AGENT_NAME> --agent-model <AGENT_MODEL> --agent-version <AGENT_VERSION>`. Foolery only forwards that returned prompt. Update Knots so lease-backed completion guidance treats the lease as the source of actor and agent identity and stops instructing clients to restate those flags.

Verification:
1. Add or update automated tests covering `kno claim --json --lease ...` and `kno poll --claim --json --lease ...`; the targeted lease-aware tests pass and assert that the completion command omits `--actor-kind`, `--agent-name`, `--agent-model`, and `--agent-version`.
2. `cargo test` passes in `/Users/cartine/knots`.
3. `rg -n -- "--agent-name <AGENT_NAME>|--agent-model <AGENT_MODEL>|--agent-version <AGENT_VERSION>|--actor-kind agent" src/poll_claim.rs loom/work_sdlc/prompts README.md` does not show those placeholders in claim/poll completion guidance or workflow step templates.

Constraints:
- The observed bad command came from `kno claim` output for `maestro-acf4`; Foolerys outer execution prompt only instructed `kno claim ... --lease ...` and then forwarded the returned prompt verbatim.
- The primary source is `src/poll_claim.rs`; the loom prompt templates and README currently reinforce the same explicit-flag pattern.
- Focus on observable prompt and completion-command behavior, not just internal refactoring.
data.title: Remove explicit agent flags from lease-backed completion prompts
